name: Order Condition for Linear Multistep Methods
statement_text:
A linear multistep method [α, β] has order p (or higher) if
and only if
                     α(exp(z)) + zβ(exp(z)) = O(z p+1 ).

 Because we have departed from the traditional (ρ, σ) formulation for linear
multistep methods, we restate this result in that standard notation:
statement_latex:
\begin{theorem}
A linear multistep method $[\alpha, \beta]$ has order $p$ (or higher) if and only if
\[
\alpha(\exp(z)) + z\beta(\exp(z)) = O(z^{p+1}).
\]
Because we have departed from the traditional $(\rho, \sigma)$ formulation for linear multistep methods, we restate this result in that standard notation:
\end{theorem}
